name: Super Tilt Bro. (NES homebrew)
_verified: 2026-06-09 — addresses SOURCE-DERIVED from GPL source game/mem_labels.asm + game/constants.asm (sgadrat/super-tilt-bro). NCI pipeline live-verified: ROM present at /userdata/roms/nes/Super_Tilt_Bro_(E).nes, RetroArch NCI :55355 responded on title screen (global_game_state=0x01 confirmed). In-game field values (stocks/positions/state) NOT yet live-verified against rendered display: menu navigation blocked because input.button (no seat) routes to the dev virtual controller, which RetroArch maps to P2, and STB title/mode-selection requires P1 input to advance. Resolution: run a server-side launch with explicit seat assignment (seat 1 = P1) or use the gose_vm_server /launch + a paired physical controller, then verify pa_stocks=4, pb_stocks=4 at match start.

launch._note: Full ROM name: Super_Tilt_Bro_(E).nes. gose_vm_server /launch takes game=Super_Tilt_Bro_(E) (no extension). The PAL/E variant is the only one present; it runs fine under the NTSC emulation (audio timing slightly off but gameplay correct).

seats._note: Player A = NES controller port 1 (left side in character select). Player B = NES controller port 2 (right side). In the GOSE virtual-pad model: if using server-launched game with explicit seats, P1 seat=1, P2 seat=2. If using the dev virtual controller (input.button, no seat), it routes to js0 = the P2 slot (right/Player B), same as in Pong — VERIFY empirically before a match. For a 2-AI match: launch via the GOSE lobby endpoint with two distinct AI tokens each pinned to a seat.

controls._note: Use input.button(button=X, action='press'|'release'). For combos, send the directional press before or simultaneous with A/B. All input defined in constants.asm CONTROLLER_INPUT_* macros.

game_winner.note: Winner player number: 0=Player A won, 1=Player B won. Only valid when global_game_state==0x02 (GAMEOVER). Written by game_logic.asm when the losing player's stocks reach 0.

player_a_x.note: P1 X pixel position (integer part). Combine with player_a_x_low (0x4a) for full subpixel precision.

player_a_y.note: P1 Y pixel position (integer part, increases downward). Combine with player_a_y_low (0x4c) for full precision.

player_a_x_screen.note: P1 screen scroll X index. 0 = on-screen. Non-zero = blasted off-screen horizontally (dying).

player_a_y_screen.note: P1 screen scroll Y index. 0 = on-screen. Non-zero = blasted off-screen vertically.

player_a_hitstun.note: P1 hitstun frames remaining (cannot act while >0).

player_a_damages.note: P1 damage percentage (0-255+). Higher = more knockback on next hit. Watch for it resetting to 0 on death/respawn.

player_a_stocks.note: P1 remaining lives. Starts at config_initial_stocks (default 4). 0 = eliminated (game ends after slow-down + screen transition).

player_a_velocity_h.note: P1 horizontal velocity integer part (signed: negative=left, positive=right). Subpixel at 0x50.

player_a_velocity_v.note: P1 vertical velocity integer part (positive=falling). Subpixel at 0x4e.

player_a_grounded.note: 0=airborne, non-zero=grounded (value is stage platform offset). Use to decide jump vs ground move.

player_a_hitbox_enabled.note: 0=no hitbox, 1=direct hitbox (P1 is attacking), 2=custom hitbox. Use to detect when P1 is mid-attack.

controller_a_btns.note: P1 gamepad bitmask current frame. Bit7=A, Bit6=B, Bit5=Select, Bit4=Start, Bit3=Up, Bit2=Down, Bit1=Left, Bit0=Right.

controller_b_btns.note: P2 gamepad bitmask current frame. Same bit layout.

screen_shake_counter.note: Non-zero during screen shake (strong hits / KOs). Game logic is normal during this; it is cosmetic only.

slow_down_counter.note: Non-zero during hit-pause (game logic pauses briefly on strong hit connection).

config_initial_stocks.note: Stock count configured before the match. Typically 4 (MAX_STOCKS). Read once at start to know the starting budget.

game_flow.winner_read: game_winner: 0=Player A, 1=Player B. Read AFTER global_game_state transitions to 0x02.
game_flow.death_detect: player_X_state == 0x02 (INNEXISTANT) — player is dead and not yet respawning. Also: player_X_stocks decrements when this happens.
game_flow.respawning: player_X_state == 0x01 (RESPAWN) or 0x03 (SPAWN) — player is returning to stage, not hittable.
game_flow.damage_reset: player_X_damages resets to 0 on death (respawn restores fresh %)
game_flow.match_start_sequence: Navigate: TITLE (0x01) -> MODE_SELECTION (0x07) -> CHARACTER_SELECTION (0x06) -> STAGE_SELECTION (0x05) -> INGAME (0x00). Use P1 (seat 1) input to navigate menus. A confirms selection. The game defaults to LOCAL 2P in mode selection.
game_flow.new_match: After GAMEOVER, press A (P1) to return to character select or mode select, then navigate back to INGAME.
game_flow.stock_count: Default 4 stocks each (MAX_STOCKS=4). Configurable at GAME_STATE_CONFIG (0x04) — config_initial_stocks ($d9) holds the value.
game_flow.hit_pause_note: During slow_down_counter > 0, RAM positions/states may not update (hit-pause freezes logic). Skip reads until slow_down_counter == 0 for clean state.
game_flow._stage_note: Stage boundaries vary. Players are KO'd by going off-screen in any direction. Use player_X_x_screen / player_X_y_screen == 0 as 'on stage' check.

ai_play_notes: READ global_game_state each cycle — only act when it == 0x00 (INGAME); otherwise the input has no game effect.
Core perception loop: read position (x, y), velocity (vel_h, vel_v), grounded, hitstun, damages, stocks for BOTH players.
Primary KO mechanic: knock the opponent off-stage (drive their x_screen or y_screen to non-zero). High damage = more knockback distance = easier to KO.
Damage % increases knockback: prioritize attacking a high-% opponent before trying to finish them off.
Approach: if grounded and horizontal distance to opponent > ~40px, run toward them (hold left/right). If close, use attacks.
Combos: A alone = jab (fast, low damage). B alone = special (slower, bigger). Direction+A or Direction+B = directional attacks.
Recovery: if your player is off-screen (x_screen or y_screen != 0), input up or up+B to recover. Stop attacking.
Hitstun: if your player's hitstun > 0, you cannot act — wait for it to reach 0 before sending inputs.

The dev virtual controller (input.button, no seat arg) maps to P2 (Player B) in a standard launch — same as Pong. Verify empirically each session: nudge a direction and watch which player_X_x changes.
For a 2-AI match: both AIs must use the GOSE seat system (seat 1 for P1, seat 2 for P2) with server-side launch. The dev controller is a single path — two AIs cannot both share it.
controller_a_btns / controller_b_btns let you observe what the opponent is inputting — useful for prediction but sampled at NES frame rate (~60Hz). NCI reads may lag a frame.
